features.summary: In Q2 of fiscal 2021, Costco reported a net income of $951 million, or $2.14 per share, up from $931 million, or $2.10 per share, a year ago. Net sales increased by 14.7% to $43.89 billion, with comparable sales growth of 13.0% globally. Membership fee income rose to $881.5 million, reflecting an 8% increase year-over-year, while renewal rates improved slightly to 91.0% in the U.S. and Canada.
features.management_outlook: Management anticipates a reduction in COVID-related expenses by over half starting March 1, 2021, due to the discontinuation of the $2 per hour premium wage. They expect to maintain competitive pricing and continue to drive sales through core merchandise and e-commerce growth, which has seen a 75% increase year-over-year. The company is optimistic about future sales and membership retention, despite challenging comparisons in the upcoming quarters.
features.risks: Costco faces risks related to supply chain disruptions, particularly from container shortages and port delays, which may impact inventory availability and sales. Additionally, inflationary pressures on commodities and labor costs could affect margins. The company also acknowledges the uncertainty surrounding the recovery of travel and food court sales as pandemic restrictions ease.
features.key_drivers: Key drivers of performance include strong sales in fresh foods, which have benefited from improved labor productivity and lower spoilage. E-commerce growth remains a significant contributor, with online sales increasing substantially. The company's focus on enhancing its membership offerings and improving operational efficiencies through technology and vertical integration are also expected to support future growth.
